desc:
A ghostly echo seems to linger here, in homage to the days when this chamber
stood at an important intersection in the dwarven kingdom of Moria. To the
north, Narvi's Smelting Hall now stands broken and unused, and below a great
Ceremonial Hall now lies silent. Dust and debris litter a floor, which must once
have seen a ceaseless passage of feet, and ancient torches hang useless in their
brackets, their precious oils long-since evaporated.
name: Stair Chamber

desc:
The road seems to come to an abrupt end as it enters this huge cavern. But, in
truth, this hall offers only a brief respite from the monotony of endless travel
along Azaghal's Way, which stretches onward, north and east from here. Standing
all around, are the forges of Narvi. Cold, and covered with the grease and grime
of long disuse, the great black forges somehow seem to retain a memory of the
volcanic heat, which once erupted from their hearts. Sooty- faced artisans once
scurried hither and thither, from anvil to master, seeking approval for their
work, before moving on to yet another task. However, now all is still and
silent. No more will the riches of Khazad-dum be processed here, and no hammer
will strike anvil in this place again.
name: Narvi's Smelting Hall

desc:
Blackened by the soot of Narvi's ancient black forges, the walls here tell the
tale of ancient times, when the smelting hall, to the west, swarmed with
activity, day and night. But now, all is still, and a chilly silence fills the
air.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
The realisation that a mountain of stone hangs overhead is perhaps a little
disconcerting to any but the children of Aule. However, reassurance can be
found, in the strong, straight walls, and the smooth stone floor; this proud
roadway is clearly a construction of Durin's folk.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
In the happier days, before the dwarves closed their doors against the legions
of Sauron, Khazad-dum was frequently visited by the elves of Eregion. And so,
although built by the dwarves, this roadway was not one constructed solely for
their use, as evidenced by the grand proportions of this tunnel.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
Massive flag-stones comprise the floor of this passageway, which was obviously
built with heavy use in mind. The ceiling is high above your head, and even the
span of two men's arms would not bridge from wall to wall. But it seems that
this tunnel has seen little recent use, judging by the fine film of dust which
carpets the ground.
name: Dark Passage

desc:
In the all-enveloping darkness, it would be easy to walk into the wall as this
passage swings from west to north. The deathly silence here seems to add to the
darkness, making it almost tangible, and the warm motionless air gives a
dismaying sense of claustrophobia to even the stout-hearted.
name: Dark Passage

desc:
The floor here is quite level, and paved with huge stone flags, as if to
reinforce it for the passage of heavy loads. An abandoned and rather badly
damaged mine car rests against the smooth stone wall, its ancient load of ore
long ago plundered, leaving only a small pile of worthless rubble beside it.
name: Dark Passage

desc:
Like a dark and long scar, this tunnel cuts a bold path through the mountain,
leading downward to the west of here, and levelling out to the east. Never
deviating from its straight course, the passageway stretches onward as far as
you can see.
name: Dark Sloping Passage

desc:
Although finely crafted, the utilitarian nature of this tunnel is obvious in the
unadorned and neatly squared tunnel walls, and the heavily flagged stone floor.
But the days in which it saw the energetic activities of a hoard of dwarven
miners are long gone, and today a crushing silence fills the air, save only for
the repeating echoes of your own footfalls.
name: Dark Sloping Passage

desc:
The shattered and rotten remains of an old mine car litter the floor here, and
its once-treasured cargo lies scattered about it, with all valuables long- since
plundered. But this senseless act of destruction has made no impact on the
tunnel itself, which retains its imposing strength and clean lines.
name: Dark Sloping Passage

desc:
Azaghal's Way traces a sweeping curve to the south here, to be interrupted by an
immense cavern, which the dwarves put to use, long ago, as a smelting hall.
Traces of that ancient activity are to be found here, in the smoky film, which
coats the walls of the tunnel, a residue of the massive fires, whose heat has
since been quenched by the cold march of time.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
A gloomy silence descends upon the roadway from the south, where it opens into a
large chamber. The impenetrable darkness of Moria somehow seems to be more
tangible here, as if it were working together with the strange silence, in an
effort to protect Azaghal's Way from the prying eyes of outsiders.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
A pressing stillness engulfs this vast chamber, and where there should be
echoing footfalls, silence reigns supreme. The cold, granite walls somehow seem
able to absorb sound, and no crack nor protrusion mars the featureless surface
of the rock; even the stone floor remains unbroken. It is tempting to conclude
that such a strange cavern can only be the result of some evil force, at work in
the rock itself.
name: Cavern of Silence

desc:
A sombre silence creeps in from the chamber to the east, and is so thick that it
seems to smother every echo. To the north and south, dark stone walls impose a
silent and guarded vigil upon this mighty roadway, as if the mountain itself
were wary of any stranger.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
Azaghal's Way climbs ever eastward. With a high ceiling, flat, featureless
walls, and a floor so smooth that it almost seems polished, this thoroughfare is
obviously one of ancient dwarven design. Yet now, it has an inescapable air of
evil, as if long tainted by the presence of Sauron's minions.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
Ominous shadows and shapes lurk all around, and the cold, grey walls seem to
watch every move. It almost seems that any who dare to venture here lose control
of their own fate, and surrender to the will of Moria.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
Westward, the roadway widens as it approaches the entrance to a large cavern.
Here, the cold, grey granite walls give way to delicate, swirling colours, which
intensify closer to the cave, drawing the eye ever to the west.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
Natural rock can sometimes play tricks on the eye, and so it is here, where each
rockface shows a myriad of colours, which seem to leap out and dance across the
chamber. Purples and reds, bright yellows and soft blues, criss- crossing and
merging, climb the walls, and spiral around huge stalagmites and stalactites. In
places, a single colour cuts a bold, yet slender, line across the back-drop of
dull, grey stone, climbing to trace delicate loops and whorls on the ceiling
above. Azaghal's Way plunges onward to the east and west, seemingly oblivious to
the spectacle, whilst to the south, a small, dark passageway cuts into the
mountain. The sweeping colours of the northern rock wall are punctuated only by
a small opening, which lets onto a stone staircase.
name: The Chamber of Rainbows

desc:
A mighty chasm opens up to the east, breaking the monotony of travel along this
seemingly endless road. Though the darkness here remains complete, a new sense
of orientation is offered by the changing air currents, and the more booming
echoes, which assail you from the east.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
This vast chasm, thousands of feet deep, was once riddled with dozens of
stairways, and crossed by a collection of wide bridging spans, which linked
great corbels and abutments, far above and way below. But, time has taken its
toll here, and as the earth has shifted over the ages, most of these great stone
stairs and walkways have been destroyed. Now all that is possible is to peer
upward, and wonder what mysterious crevices and tunnels hide aloft, or to gaze
into the depths, and imagine the riches which might be hoarded there. For no
stair now travels the chasm from here, and the only paths to choose are along
Azaghal's Way, to the west, or eastward across the one narrow stone bridge,
which still spans this gulf.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
The dwarves of Khazad-dum once climbed freely from level to level in this vast
shaft, which stretches north to south, and up and down, far into the darkness.
But then, in their greed, they delved too deep, setting loose a foe beyond their
match, and all their proud work was laid to waste. Where once slender bridges of
stone reached out across the abyss to a mighty granite coloumn, at the centre of
the chasm, now only shattered ruins remain. And the spiral stair, which winds
round the tower, is now quite impossible to reach.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
Booming echoes resound from the gaping rift to the west, and a refreshing
draught of cooler air breaks into the stifling stagnancy of the passageway.
Azaghal's Way turns to the north here, in a majestic, sweeping curve, and
marches off into the darkness.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
Azaghal's Way plunges eastward through the mountain, as far as the eye can see.
But at this small intersection, it curves to the south, before continuing its
inexorable westward march. To the north, a small tunnel finds its own path
through the unforgiving rock, but it too boasts the smooth stone walls which
typify the work of the dwarves of Moria.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
The walls of this small tunnel are smooth and flawless. Gently curving, they
give this small passageway an almost circular cross-section, except where they
meet the floor, which is flat and level, with no pits or protrusions.
name: A Small Tunnel

desc:
Massive aggregates of small, prismatic, indigo and azure crystals completely
cover the walls and ceiling of this pretty cave. Even the floor is blue,
although its crystalline surface has been worn quite smooth. All that can be
seen interrupting this endless cerulean sea are some scattered bands of green
malachite, which nestle amongst the azurite.
name: Azure Cave

desc:
Empty and silent, the road stretches east and west. In the inky darkness, it is
easy to succumb to all manner of wild and terrifying fantasies. These products
of imagination are given greater reality by a sense of evil, which seems to
reside in the cold, grey granite of the walls, floor and ceiling.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
In the ancient days of the First and Second Ages of the Sun, before the years of
Sauron's dominion, this imposing thoroughfare must have echoed day and night to
the activity of the busy dwarves, and the innumerable footfalls of the hosts of
travellers, who passed this way, to trade with the wealthy overlords of
Khazad-dum. But now all is cold and silent.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
The road stretches off into the darkness to the east and west. At one point it
must have been a major artery of dwarven traffic. Now through eons of disuse a
thick layer of dust has accumulated in the corners and gutters.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
Two major roads come to an intersection. At one time it must have been bustling
with traffic. Now the dark is its travelling companion. The ceiling comes to a
slight peak forming a quadripartite vault. To the north and south twinkling
lights can sometimes be glimpsed.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
The sparkling canopy of blueish rock studded with diamonds simulating a starry
night ends in a greyish rock that remarkably resembles a cloud blotting out the
night. The road continues south though joining another smaller road.
name: Dimrill Road

desc:
The road continues in a north south direction followed by the twinkling canopy
above. The canopy appears to be a blueish rock with jools and diamonds imbedded
in it. There is a large opening to the west where a door used to be. The door
has been wrenched from its hinges and lies in several pieces to the right of the
doorway.
name: Dimrill Road

desc:
A simple one room home like the ruins of the ones that pepper the road outside.
This one seems to have escaped the destruction that befell the others. The house
still has that lived in look as pieces of detritus and debris litter the area.
Much of the east wall has been knocked out and the resulting rubble removed
creating a serviceable doorway.
name: Old Dwarven Home

desc:
A wide road which joins an intersection to the west. It runs straight for quite
a while in both directions. A smaller road branches off to the south.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
This small simple hallway spills out onto a large road to the west. There is
little decoration or masonry evident. The natural lay of the rock seems to shine
forth in the simplicity of the design.
name: Hallway of Aule

desc:
The road gives a very convincing illusion of being outside. The craftwork of the
dwarves is never more evident than here. Through clever tricks of masonry, the
roof soars, clothed in the blue of eternal night, lit by diamonds and blue gems
which do a more than credible job of imitating the twinkling canopy of stars. A
small hallway leads off to the east.
name: Dimrill Road

desc:
The road leads out of the valley with the same name. The road is wide and paved
offering easy travel. The road continues off to the south and bends to the east.
name: Dimrill Road

desc:
The road widens a bit here as if indicating that this road was to become a major
thouroughfare. However, it immediately narrows to the south as if the builders
changed their mind.
name: Dwarven Road

desc:
The road continues to the north and south. The walls are bare and bereft of any
adornment. The hard rock has been polished smooth, not on purpose but by the
passing of a multitude of hands.
name: Dwarven Road

desc:
This is a small road that lies in disrepair. The gutters are choked with small
stones and other debris which occasionally spill out onto the road proper,
making footing treacherous in spots.
name: On a Dwarven Road

desc:
A large greyish cloud fashioned from the natural grey rock of the ceiling to the
north gives forth to a starry sky made up of a deep blue marble sprinkled
liberally with twinkling gems which reflect the available light. The road
continues to the south.
name: Dimrill Road

desc:
A large, echoing tunnel slopes away to the east in an unwavering path, so
straight that it can only have been the work of the dwarven master builders. The
quality of the workmanship is evident in the smooth, squared sides of the
passage, and the firm, even feel of the floor beneath your feet.
name: Dark Sloping Passage

desc:
The bright array of colours shining into this tunnel from the chamber to the
north barely break the gloom. Yet somehow your light seems brighter and stronger
when you look to the north, compared to when you turn and gaze up this narrow
tunnel to the south.
name: Dark Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel here describes a ninety degree turn, climbing away from you to the
west, falling off gently to the north. The walls, ceiling and floor here are
quite smooth, but as the slope is so gentle you have little trouble with your
footing, despite the engulfing darkness.
name: Dark Sloping Tunnel

desc:
A wide stone staircase leads down from here, spiralling into the darkness. Each
stair is badly chipped and worn, with flaked edges, and uneven hollows toward
the centre, and part way down, a huge block of masonry obstructs the way.
name: Stairwell

desc:
Progress along this imposing roadway seems woefully slow. The great breadth of
the passage, and its high ceiling, make it appear foreshortened, contributing
further to the feeling that despite striding wearily onward, no distance has
been covered.
name: Azaghal's Way

desc:
Azaghal's Way marches steadily onward through the mighty Hithaeglir, ascending
steadily as it progresses eastward. Unmarked by the passage of time, this broad
thoroughfare retains the clean lines and solid feel, bestowed upon it by its
dwarven architects, so many years ago.
name: Azaghal's Way
